  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For this improvement pass, should I A) focus strictly on sentence-level copyediting and concision while preserving the current content and order, or B) also perform paragraph/section reordering and light reframing of the contribution/claims (e.g., introduce the method before results and tighten the narrative)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.5": "Improving the draft likely benefits from reordering content (e.g., presenting the method before results) and tightening the cross-paragraph narrative.",
    "R1.6": "The request strongly implies copyediting for grammar, clarity, and concision at the sentence and paragraph level.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should explicitly signal uncertainty where claims (e.g., ‘competitive’) depend on indirectly comparable baselines.",
    "RX.2": "Language must avoid overstating results relative to what is reported (e.g., hedge comparative claims appropriately).",
    "RX.3": "Work will remain within the scope of improving the provided text without adding new experimental content or citations.",
    "RX.4": "Careful phrasing reduces the risk of misleading readers about performance or evaluation comparability.",
    "RX.5": "Revisions should maintain traceability to the original statements, noting where changes are substantive versus cosmetic."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this improvement pass, should I A) focus strictly on sentence-level copyediting and concision while preserving the current content and order, or B) also perform paragraph/section reordering and light reframing of the contribution/claims (e.g., introduce the method before results and tighten the narrative)?

rationale.R1.5: Improving the draft likely benefits from reordering content (e.g., presenting the method before results) and tightening the cross-paragraph narrative.
rationale.R1.6: The request strongly implies copyediting for grammar, clarity, and concision at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should explicitly signal uncertainty where claims (e.g., ‘competitive’) depend on indirectly comparable baselines.
rationale.RX.2: Language must avoid overstating results relative to what is reported (e.g., hedge comparative claims appropriately).
rationale.RX.3: Work will remain within the scope of improving the provided text without adding new experimental content or citations.
rationale.RX.4: Careful phrasing reduces the risk of misleading readers about performance or evaluation comparability.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions should maintain traceability to the original statements, noting where changes are substantive versus cosmetic.
